turns.svg:
<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='512' height='512' viewBox='0 0 512 512'>
  <rect width='100%' height='100%' fill='#f8fbff' />
  <!-- tail -->
  <path d='M360 200 L470 140 L450 170 L510 160 L430 240 L470 260 L400 300 L420 270 L360 230 Z' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#5C3A21' stroke-width='10' stroke-linejoin='round' />
  <path d='M400 240 L420 260 L390 275 Z' fill='#5C3A21' />
  <!-- body -->
  <ellipse cx='256' cy='360' rx='140' ry='110' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#000' stroke-width='4' />
  <!-- head -->
  <circle cx='256' cy='240' r='120' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#000' stroke-width='4' />
  <!-- ears -->
  <path d='M196 150 L162 18 L184 20 L220 140 Z' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#000' stroke-width='4' stroke-linejoin='round' />
  <polygon points='172,22 188,22 208,76 172,150' fill='#000' opacity='1' />
  <path d='M316 150 L350 18 L328 20 L292 140 Z' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#000' stroke-width='4' stroke-linejoin='round' />
  <polygon points='344,22 328,22 308,76 344,150' fill='#000' opacity='1' />
  <!-- cheeks -->
  <circle cx='200' cy='288' r='28' fill='#ff3b3b' />
  <circle cx='312' cy='288' r='28' fill='#ff3b3b' />
  <!-- eyes -->
  <ellipse cx='230' cy='224' rx='18' ry='22' fill='#000' />
  <ellipse cx='282' cy='224' rx='18' ry='22' fill='#000' />
  <circle cx='238' cy='214' r='6' fill='#fff' />
  <circle cx='290' cy='214' r='6' fill='#fff' />
  <!-- nose and mouth -->
  <circle cx='256' cy='256' r='6' fill='#000' />
  <path d='M244 270 Q256 282 268 270' stroke='#000' stroke-width='4' fill='none' stroke-linecap='round' />
  <!-- arms -->
  <path d='M170 320 Q190 300 210 310 Q190 330 170 320 Z' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#000' stroke-width='3' />
  <path d='M342 320 Q322 300 302 310 Q322 330 342 320 Z' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#000' stroke-width='3' />
  <!-- feet -->
  <ellipse cx='220' cy='440' rx='28' ry='12' fill='#5C3A21' />
  <ellipse cx='292' cy='440' rx='28' ry='12' fill='#5C3A21' />
  <!-- back stripes -->
  <path d='M200 220 Q240 200 270 215 Q235 210 200 240 Z' fill='#5C3A21' opacity='0.95' />
  <path d='M280 200 Q320 180 340 210 Q310 195 280 220 Z' fill='#5C3A21' opacity='0.95' />
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

The eyes will be black ovals with small white highlights, and I’ll add red circles for cheeks, a small black mouth, and a tiny black triangle for the nose. The arms will be small rounded rectangles, while the feet will be ovals in brown. I need to ensure the tail has a zigzag shape filled with yellow and a brown base, keeping it clean and simple without any text or numbers. {"svg":"<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='512' height='512' viewBox='0 0 512 512'>
  <rect width='100%' height='100%' fill='#f8fbff' />
  <!-- tail -->
  <path d='M360 200 L470 140 L450 170 L510 160 L430 240 L470 260 L400 300 L420 270 L360 230 Z' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#5C3A21' stroke-width='10' stroke-linejoin='round' />
  <path d='M400 240 L420 260 L390 275 Z' fill='#5C3A21' />
  <!-- body -->
  <ellipse cx='256' cy='360' rx='140' ry='110' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#000' stroke-width='4' />
  <!-- head -->
  <circle cx='256' cy='240' r='120' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#000' stroke-width='4' />
  <!-- ears -->
  <path d='M196 150 L162 18 L184 20 L220 140 Z' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#000' stroke-width='4' stroke-linejoin='round' />
  <polygon points='172,22 188,22 208,76 172,150' fill='#000' opacity='1' />
  <path d='M316 150 L350 18 L328 20 L292 140 Z' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#000' stroke-width='4' stroke-linejoin='round' />
  <polygon points='344,22 328,22 308,76 344,150' fill='#000' opacity='1' />
  <!-- cheeks -->
  <circle cx='200' cy='288' r='28' fill='#ff3b3b' />
  <circle cx='312' cy='288' r='28' fill='#ff3b3b' />
  <!-- eyes -->
  <ellipse cx='230' cy='224' rx='18' ry='22' fill='#000' />
  <ellipse cx='282' cy='224' rx='18' ry='22' fill='#000' />
  <circle cx='238' cy='214' r='6' fill='#fff' />
  <circle cx='290' cy='214' r='6' fill='#fff' />
  <!-- nose and mouth -->
  <circle cx='256' cy='256' r='6' fill='#000' />
  <path d='M244 270 Q256 282 268 270' stroke='#000' stroke-width='4' fill='none' stroke-linecap='round' />
  <!-- arms -->
  <path d='M170 320 Q190 300 210 310 Q190 330 170 320 Z' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#000' stroke-width='3' />
  <path d='M342 320 Q322 300 302 310 Q322 330 342 320 Z' fill='#FFD032' stroke='#000' stroke-width='3' />
  <!-- feet -->
  <ellipse cx='220' cy='440' rx='28' ry='12' fill='#5C3A21' />
  <ellipse cx='292' cy='440' rx='28' ry='12' fill='#5C3A21' />
  <!-- back stripes -->
  <path d='M200 220 Q240 200 270 215 Q235 210 200 240 Z' fill='#5C3A21' opacity='0.95' />
  <path d='M280 200 Q320 180 340 210 Q310 195 280 220 Z' fill='#5C3A21' opacity='0.95' />
</svg>"}
message.reasoning:
**Creating SVG for Pikachu**

I need to produce an SVG drawing of Pikachu, but without any text, labels, or numbers since it's essential to keep it simple. Pikachu is a copyrighted character from Pokémon, but it seems I can still draw it as long as I follow the guidelines. The design will consist of basic SVG shapes, using a color palette that captures Pikachu's essence. I’ll aim for a 512x512 canvas, ensuring everything stays valid and straightforward while keeping it recognizable.**Designing Pikachu SVG Details**

I'm crafting an SVG drawing of Pikachu using various simple shapes. The body will be an oval in yellow, with a circular head, and triangular ears that have black tips. The eyes will be black ovals with small white highlights, and I’ll add red circles for cheeks, a small black mouth, and a tiny black triangle for the nose. The arms will be small rounded rectangles, while the feet will be ovals in brown. I need to ensure the tail has a zigzag shape filled with yellow and a brown base, keeping it clean and simple without any text or numbers. I'll finalize the SVG string while remembering to properly format it for JSON.**Constructing the SVG Format**

I find it simpler to include newline characters in my SVG string while ensuring quotes are properly escaped. I’ll use double quotes for the JSON key and value but switch to single quotes for the SVG attributes, which avoids extra escaping. I’ll start building the SVG with a light sky background (though I might skip this) and focus on the tail shape. For the tail, I’ll use a path with a yellow fill and brown stroke, keeping it straightforward. Let's proceed with defining the tail's path structure.**Finalizing Pikachu SVG Elements**

I need to make sure the path for the tail is closed and uses a yellow fill (#FFD032), with a stroke of '#5C3A21' and a stroke width of 12, plus round joins for smoothness. For the body shadow, I'll draw a large circle for the head centered at 256, 220 with a radius of 120. The fill will be '#FFD032', and I might keep the black outline minimal. The ears will be elongated triangles, filled similarly, but I’ll simplify their shape using paths. I’ll add the distinctive black eyes, red cheeks, a small nose, and a curved mouth, along with small arms and brown stripes on the back, all arranged to show Pikachu facing forward!**Assembling the SVG for Pikachu**

I’m carefully crafting the coordinates for Pikachu's SVG. The canvas will be centered at 256,256. I'll draw the head as a circle, with the body as an oval below. For the ears, I'll create paths that point to the top of the head, ensuring they mirror each other. Adding features like black eyes, red cheeks, a small black nose, and a simple mouth is crucial too. The feet will be small ovals, and the tail will have a big zigzag shape, filled with yellow and stroked in brown. Finally, I'll make sure the JSON string is compact, with double quotes around the key and single quotes inside the SVG, escaping any newlines as necessary!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

**Refining spellings and suggestions**

I’m thinking about how to spell "pikachu." It's a proper noun, so I suppose it should be lowercase. Since the developer indicated one-word guesses with no spaces, I’ll start with my top suggestions: pikachu, pokemon, raichu, and pichu. Then there are terms like electric, lightning, and mouse, all of which are relevant. I need 20 one-word guesses, so I’ll ensure they are distinct as well, like chibi and kawaii, which fit the context.**Creating ordered list for JSON**

I’m organizing a list of 20 one-word guesses. The words include pikachu, pokemon, pichu, raichu, electric, and lightning, among others like mouse, yellow, and cheeks. I’ll double-check that none are repeated and that all are one word without spaces. And of course, it's fine to use "pikachu" since it's an animated character identification. Now, I'll prepare the JSON format with exactly 20 strings contained in the "guesses" field using proper syntax. Let's finalize this!
